fields.name: Sabarimala
fields.tagline: Sacred Pilgrimage Shrine of Lord Ayyappa
fields.about:
Sabarimala, located in the Periyar Tiger Reserve of Pathanamthitta district,
      is one of the most significant pilgrimage centers in India. Dedicated to
      Lord Ayyappa, the temple attracts millions of devotees every year,
      especially during the Mandala-Makaravilakku season (November to January).
      The temple is situated amidst dense forests and hills, offering a unique
      spiritual and natural experience.
fields.highlights:
Annual Mandala & Makaravilakku festival
Sacred 18 holy steps (Pathinettam Padi)
Forest trekking experience
Millions of devotees annually
fields.essential_info:
Virtual queue booking required during peak season
Online booking via official Devaswom website
Traditional dress code applies
Open during specific months only
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Pamba (base camp)
Railway: Chengannur (approx. 90 km)
Airport: Trivandrum (approx. 170 km)
From Pamba, pilgrims trek about 5 km uphill to reach the temple.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Medical centers available at Pamba
KSRTC transport services during season
Accommodation facilities at Pamba & Nilakkal
Parking & emergency services available

fields.name: Anjumala Para
fields.tagline: A Scenic Hilltop Escape in Pathanamthitta
fields.about:
Anjumala Para is a scenic hilltop destination in Pathanamthitta district,
    known for its open rocky terrain and panoramic countryside views.
    It is a peaceful spot ideal for those looking to escape busy town life
    and enjoy fresh air and natural beauty.
The name �Anjumala� literally means �Five Hills,� referring to the
    surrounding hill formations that enhance its scenic charm.
fields.highlights:
Wide-angle valley views
Rocky hilltop landscape
Sunrise and sunset viewpoints
Cool breeze and open sky scenery
fields.essential_info:
No entry fee
Ideal for photography and nature walks
Best time: October to February
Visit early morning or evening
Limited facilities on hilltop
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Pathanamthitta
Railway Station: Chengannur (approx. 30 km)
Airport: Trivandrum International Airport (approx. 120 km)
Accessible by local roads; private vehicle recommended.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Restaurants and shops in nearby towns
Petrol pumps within 5�10 km
Hospitals in Pathanamthitta town
No permanent stalls at hilltop

fields.name: Aranmula
fields.tagline: Heritage Village on the Banks of River Pamba
fields.about:
Aranmula is a historic and culturally rich village in Pathanamthitta district,
      situated along the banks of River Pamba. It is widely known for the
      Aranmula Parthasarathy Temple and the famous Aranmula Vallamkali
      (Snake Boat Race). The village beautifully blends spirituality,
      traditional art forms, and scenic river views, making it a must-visit
      heritage destination.
fields.highlights:
The Aranmula Vallamkali is one of Kerala�s oldest traditional boat races,
          held during Onam season. It is more of a devotional event than a competition.
          The temple is also one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Lord Krishna.
fields.essential_info:
Temple follows traditional dress code
Best time to visit: August�September (Boat race season)
Temple timings vary during festivals
Parking available near temple premises
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Pathanamthitta (approx. 10 km)
Railway Station: Chengannur (approx. 12 km)
Airport: Trivandrum International Airport (approx. 120 km)
Regular bus and taxi services are available from nearby towns.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Hotels and lodges available in Pathanamthitta & Chengannur
Restaurants near temple area
Petrol pumps within 5�10 km radius
Hospitals in Pathanamthitta town

fields.name: Charalkkunnu Hill Station
fields.tagline: A Scenic Viewpoint Overlooking the Pamba Valley
fields.about:
Charalkkunnu is a picturesque hill station located near Kozhencherry in
    Pathanamthitta district. Known for its breathtaking views of the Pamba River
    and surrounding valleys, it is a popular spot for nature lovers and photographers.
    The hilltop offers a calm and refreshing atmosphere, making it ideal for
    short trips, family outings, and peaceful sunset views.
fields.highlights:
Panoramic view of Pamba River
Lush green valleys and hills
Beautiful sunrise and sunset views
Cool breeze and open viewpoints
fields.essential_info:
Best time to visit: October to March
No major entry fee
Limited food stalls nearby
Evening visits recommended for sunset
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Kozhencherry (approx. 5 km)
Railway Station: Chengannur (approx. 25 km)
Airport: Cochin International Airport (approx. 115 km)
Accessible by road; private vehicles recommended.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Hotels and restaurants in Kozhencherry
Petrol pumps within 5�10 km
Hospitals in Kozhencherry town
Small local shops nearby

fields.name: Chengara View Point
fields.about:
Chengara Viewpoint is a scenic hilltop location offering expansive
      panoramic views of valleys, plantations, and distant hills in
      Pathanamthitta district. Known for its calm surroundings and refreshing
      breeze, the viewpoint is ideal for short trips, photography, and peaceful
      evening visits. It remains relatively less crowded compared to major
      tourist destinations, making it perfect for those seeking quiet nature.
fields.highlights:
A short walk may be required from the parking spot to reach
          the main viewing area. The terrain includes uneven rocky patches,
          so appropriate footwear is advisable.
There is generally no entry fee. However, visitors should respect
      local community guidelines and avoid disturbing agricultural lands
      or private property.
fields.essential_info:
No major commercial facilities at the top
Best visited during early morning or late evening
Road access may include narrow village routes
Carry water and basic necessities
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Pathanamthitta
Accessible by private vehicles and two-wheelers.
Some stretches may be steep; careful driving recommended.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Restaurants and shops in nearby towns
Petrol pumps within short driving distance
Hospitals and medical facilities in Pathanamthitta

fields.name: Chuttippara
fields.tagline: A Quiet Rocky Hill with Panoramic Views
fields.about:
Chuttippara is a peaceful rocky hilltop destination in Pathanamthitta district,
    known for its open landscape and wide countryside views. It is a hidden spot
    preferred by locals for short evening visits and photography.

    The hill offers a calm atmosphere, making it a great place to relax,
    enjoy cool breezes, and experience scenic sunsets away from crowded
    tourist centers.
fields.highlights:
Panoramic village and valley views
Open rocky hilltop terrain
Beautiful sunset scenery
Peaceful environment
fields.essential_info:
No entry fee
Best time: October to February
Evening visits recommended
No permanent shops on hilltop
Wear proper footwear
Be cautious near rock edges
Carry drinking water
Avoid visiting alone at night
Do not litter the area
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Pathanamthitta
Railway Station: Chengannur (approx. 30 km)
Airport: Trivandrum International Airport (approx. 120 km)
Accessible by local roads; private vehicles preferred.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Restaurants in nearby town areas
Petrol pumps within 5�10 km
Hospitals in Pathanamthitta town
No restrooms on hilltop

fields.name: Gavi
fields.tagline: Eco-Tourism Paradise in the Western Ghats
fields.about:
Gavi is a serene eco-tourism destination located inside the Periyar Tiger Reserve
      in Pathanamthitta district. Surrounded by dense forests, grasslands, cardamom plantations,
      and hills, Gavi offers a peaceful escape into nature. It is one of the few places
      in Kerala where visitors can experience wildlife, trekking, and lake-side views
      in an undisturbed forest setting.
fields.highlights:
Gavi Lake and boating experience
Forest safari inside Periyar Tiger Reserve
Bird watching (over 260 species recorded)
Panoramic viewpoints of Western Ghats
Cardamom and tea plantations
fields.essential_info:
Entry permitted only with forest department approval
Limited daily visitor passes
Best time to visit: September to March
Mobile network coverage is weak
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Vandiperiyar (approx. 28 km)
Railway Station: Kottayam (approx. 140 km)
Airport: Kochi International Airport (approx. 170 km)
Visitors usually enter through Angamoozhy or Vallakkadavu forest check posts.
fields.nearby_facilities: Only limited vehicles are allowed daily to maintain ecological balance.

fields.name: Konni-Adavi
fields.about:
Konni is famously known as the "Land of Elephants" and is home to the historic
    Konni Elephant Training Center. Located amidst forested hills in Pathanamthitta district,
    Konni offers a mix of wildlife, eco-tourism, and scenic beauty.
    Adavi, located nearby, is an eco-tourism destination managed by the forest department,
    offering bamboo rafting, coracle boating, and nature walks along the Kallar river.
    Together, Konni and Adavi provide a refreshing nature-based travel experience.
fields.highlights:
Historic elephant training center
Elephant feeding & interaction sessions
Elephant museum
Forest surroundings
Bamboo rafting
Coracle boating
Nature trails
River-side relaxation spots
fields.safety_tips:
Follow forest department instructions strictly
Avoid feeding animals without permission
Wear life jackets during boating
Do not litter; protect wildlife habitat
Travel during daytime for better safety
fields.entry_booking:
Entry fee applicable at Elephant Camp
Boating tickets available at Adavi counter
Peak season may require early arrival
Forest department regulations apply
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Konni
Railway Station: Chengannur (approx. 35 km)
Airport: Trivandrum International Airport (approx. 110 km)
Well connected by road; buses and taxis available.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Restaurants and shops in Konni town
Petrol pumps within 5 km radius
Hospitals in Pathanamthitta town
Parking facilities available

fields.name: Mannera Waterfall
fields.about:
Mannera Waterfall is a calm and lesser-known natural stream located in
Pathanamthitta district. Surrounded by greenery and rural landscapes,
it offers a quiet escape from crowded tourist locations.
fields.highlights:
Natural rocky stream
Ideal for short visits & photography
Peaceful countryside surroundings
fields.essential_info:
Best time: Post-monsoon
Limited facilities nearby
Carry drinking water
fields.safety_tips:
Rocks may be slippery
Avoid heavy rain visits
Do not litter

fields.name: Nilakkal Mahadeva Temple
fields.about:
Nilakkal Mahadeva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Shiva,
    located near the base camp of Sabarimala in Pathanamthitta district.
    Surrounded by dense forests, the temple holds spiritual significance
    for devotees visiting Sabarimala.
    It is believed to be centuries old and is associated with the ancient
    trade routes that once passed through this region. The peaceful forest
    setting adds to its sacred atmosphere.
fields.highlights:
Dedicated to Lord Shiva
Visited by Sabarimala pilgrims
Important during Mandala-Makaravilakku season
Peaceful forest surroundings
Traditional Kerala temple architecture
Simple yet serene shrine
Close proximity to Nilakkal Christian Church
Forest landscape and calm environment
fields.essential_info:
Open during Sabarimala pilgrimage season
Temple timings may vary seasonally
Modest traditional dress recommended
Basic facilities available at Nilakkal base camp
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Location: Nilakkal Base Camp
Railway Station: Chengannur (approx. 85 km)
Airport: Trivandrum International Airport (approx. 150 km)
Accessible by road via Sabarimala route.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Parking facilities at Nilakkal base camp
Temporary shops during pilgrimage season
Medical camps during peak season
Rest areas for pilgrims

fields.name: Orakkanppara
fields.tagline: A Hidden Scenic Rock Viewpoint in Pathanamthitta
fields.about:
Orakkanpara is a lesser-known scenic hill and rocky viewpoint located in
      Pathanamthitta district. Surrounded by lush greenery and rolling hills,
      it offers panoramic views of the countryside. Unlike crowded tourist
      destinations, Orakkanpara remains peaceful and relatively unexplored,
      making it ideal for nature lovers and photography enthusiasts.
fields.highlights:
Wide hilltop views of surrounding valleys
Beautiful sunrise and sunset scenery
Open rocky terrain ideal for photography
Peaceful and less crowded atmosphere
Natural greenery and fresh air
fields.essential_info:
No major commercial facilities nearby
Best visited during early morning or evening
Carry water and essentials
Limited mobile network coverage
fields.trekking_info:
Visitors may need to walk a short distance from the parking area
          to reach the main viewpoint. The rocky surface can be uneven,
          so comfortable footwear is recommended. No marked trekking routes
          are officially maintained.
fields.safety_tips:
Avoid visiting during heavy rain due to slippery rocks
Be cautious near cliff edges
Do not litter; maintain ecological cleanliness
Travel in groups during late hours
 sufficient water and basic supplies
fields.entry_booking:
There is generally no formal ticketing system. However, visitors are
      advised to check local guidelines and respect private property or
      restricted areas if any.
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Pathanamthitta
Accessible by local roads; private vehicle recommended.
Some parts may require short uphill walking.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Restaurants and shops available in Pathanamthitta town
Petrol pumps within town limits
Hospitals and medical facilities in Pathanamthitta

fields.name: Pandalam Valiyakoikkal Temple
fields.about:
Pandalam Valiyakoikkal Temple is a historically and spiritually significant temple
    located in Pandalam town of Pathanamthitta district. It is closely associated with
    Lord Ayyappa and the royal family of Pandalam.
    According to legend, Lord Ayyappa was raised as the prince of the Pandalam royal
    family. The temple complex and nearby Pandalam Palace are important pilgrimage
    spots for devotees before proceeding to Sabarimala.
fields.highlights:
Traditional Kerala temple architecture
Nearby Pandalam Palace
Sacred ornaments (Thiruvabharanam) procession
Peaceful riverside setting
Associated with Lord Ayyappa�s childhood
Part of Sabarimala pilgrimage tradition
Royal family temple of Pandalam
Important during Mandala season
fields.essential_info:
Open daily for devotees
Modest traditional attire recommended
Heavy crowd during Mandala season
Special rituals during festival periods
fields.safety_tips:
Maintain temple decorum and silence
Follow instructions from temple authorities
Avoid littering the premises
Be cautious during festival crowd rush
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Pandalam
Railway Station: Chengannur (approx. 20 km)
Airport: Trivandrum International Airport (approx. 110 km)
Well connected by road from Pathanamthitta and Chengannur.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Hotels and lodges in Pandalam town
Restaurants and tea shops nearby
Petrol pumps within town limits
Hospitals in nearby towns

fields.name: Parumala Church
fields.tagline: Sacred Pilgrimage Centre of Saint Geevarghese Mar Gregorios
fields.about:
Parumala Church, officially known as St. Peter�s and St. Paul�s Orthodox Church,
    is one of the most important pilgrimage centers of the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church.
    Located on the banks of River Pamba near Thiruvalla, it is the final resting place of
    Saint Geevarghese Mar Gregorios (Parumala Thirumeni).
  
    The church attracts thousands of devotees throughout the year, especially during
    the annual feast held in November. The peaceful riverside setting adds to the
    spiritual atmosphere of the place.
fields.highlights:
Modern circular church structure
Beautiful altar and stained-glass interiors
Riverside landscape
Well-maintained prayer hall
Tomb of Saint Geevarghese Mar Gregorios
Major pilgrimage site for Orthodox Christians
Annual feast in November
Daily prayers and special services
fields.essential_info:
Open daily for visitors and prayer
Modest dress recommended
Peak crowd during November feast
Photography may be restricted inside
fields.safety_tips:
Maintain silence inside prayer hall
Respect religious customs
Follow instructions during feast days
Avoid crowding near sacred tomb area
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Thiruvalla (approx. 10 km)
Railway Station:  Thiruvalla Railway Station
Airport: Cochin International Airport (approx. 110 km)
Well connected by road with regular bus services.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Hotels and lodges in Thiruvalla town
Restaurants and bakeries nearby
Petrol pumps within 5�10 km radius
Hospitals in Thiruvalla

fields.name: Perunthenaruvi
fields.tagline: Scenic Waterfall Along the River Pamba
fields.about:
Perunthenaruvi Waterfalls is a beautiful and serene waterfall located
      near Vechoochira in Pathanamthitta district. The name literally means
      "Great Honey Stream." The waterfall flows over rocky terrain along the
      River Pamba, creating wide cascading streams rather than a single drop.
      It is an ideal destination for nature lovers, photographers, and families
      looking for a peaceful outing.
fields.highlights:
Wide rock formations with flowing water streams
Beautiful sunset views
Calm picnic atmosphere
Forest surroundings and fresh air
Photography-friendly landscape
fields.essential_info:
Best time to visit: Post-monsoon (September�January)
Water level increases during heavy rainfall
Entry regulated by local authorities
Carry drinking water and essentials
fields.trekking_info:
A short walking trail leads visitors to the waterfall viewpoints.
          The rocks can be slippery, especially during monsoon season.
          Visitors are advised to wear proper footwear while exploring.
fields.safety_tips:
Avoid entering deep water zones
Rocks can be extremely slippery
Do not visit during heavy rainfall alerts
Keep children under supervision
Avoid littering; maintain ecological cleanliness
fields.entry_booking:
A small entry fee may be collected by local authorities for maintenance.
      Timings may vary depending on weather and safety conditions.
      During heavy monsoon, entry may be restricted.
fields.how_to_reach:
Nearest Town: Ranni (approx. 10 km)
Railway Station: Chengannur (approx. 45 km)
Airport: Trivandrum International Airport (approx. 110 km)
Well-connected by road; parking facilities available near the site.
fields.nearby_facilities:
Small shops and eateries near entrance
Petrol pumps in Ranni town
Hospitals available in Ranni & Pathanamthitta
Basic parking facilities available

fields.review_text: It's a nice place, i've been there last month with family, great view though.
